negotiated_from: The actual problem (fanned-out but shallow generation), readers (Phase 4 + coverage maintainers, operator, release owner), constraints (no live calls, no weakened validators, benchmark quarantine, baseline/enhancement compatibility), and risk (intentional pass/fail flip on an existing path).

gates.requirement: Source-derived depth budget exists and is deterministic.
gates.evidence: libs/writing/depth_budget.py with a frozen injectable DepthPolicy, derive_section_depth_budget, evaluate_plan_depth, render_depth_budget_lines; every number derives from mapped evidence / packets / content blocks / required topics / token bank / source handles; byte-identical on rerun.
gates.verify: Unit tests assert derivation values and rerun byte-equality; comparator file never imported.

gates.requirement: Shallow-but-covered claim plan FAILS; sufficiently detailed one PASSES.
gates.evidence: claim_plan.validate_claim_plan emits required_topic_underfilled_for_mapped_evidence and section_claim_density_below_source_floor with measured-vs-required detail. (Content-block coverage stays the downstream generated-coverage gate; the depth gate deliberately does not duplicate it — see repair-log R6.)
gates.verify: tests/test_phase4_depth_budget.py: shallow plan fails, detailed plan passes, single-mapped-evidence topic passes with one claim.

gates.requirement: No existing validator weakened; existing pass cases keep passing.
gates.evidence: depth_budget is an optional keyword (default None) on validate_claim_plan and the prompt builders; baseline/enhancement never compute it.
gates.verify: Full focused suites + full suite green; the ops expanded grounded command E2E still passes (1 mapped evidence id, 1 claim).

gates.requirement: Prompt carries the depth budget; system instruction asks for enough claims; no benchmark leakage.
gates.evidence: render_depth_budget_lines output embedded in the claim-plan prompt; hardened CLAIM_PLAN_SYSTEM_INSTRUCTION.
gates.verify: Prompt test asserts the depth-budget lines and per-topic targets appear and contain no benchmark string.

gates.requirement: Depth metrics are visible to operators.
gates.evidence: Per-section grounded audit block records the depth budget + measured per-topic and total claim counts.
gates.verify: Grounded E2E asserts the grounded metadata block carries depth fields.

gates.requirement: Benchmark quarantine + Phase 3 protected spec preserved.
gates.evidence: No read of ragflow-deepwiki.md anywhere in src; no change to docs/specs/protected/PHASE3_EVIDENCE_RETRIEVAL_SPEC.md or Phase 1/2/3 code.
gates.verify: git diff --exit-code on the protected spec; grep shows no comparator import in src/.

gates.evidence: py_compile clean; no mutable defaults; no import-time side effects; explicit typing; narrow exceptions; only intended deliverables changed; .sequence/ git-ignored.
gates.verify: Static checks in .sequence/static-analysis.json; git diff --check clean.

gates.requirement: TDD is assembled, validated against this DoD by a separate validator step, and repairs (if any) are logged.
gates.verify: Phase 6 validation-report status == pass with every DoD gate addressed.

accepted_tradeoffs: A previously-passing fanned-out-but-shallow expanded GROUNDED claim plan now fails the depth gate. This is the intended correction (mirrors the prior phase's accepted breadth trade-off). It is off by default at the command level (baseline), opt-in via expanded+grounded, and reversible.
out_of_scope: Any change to baseline/enhancement, Phase 1/2/3, or the protected spec
